rules.description: Always identify the higher timeframe (daily) bias before analyzing intraday setups
A market structure shift (MSS) requires a candle close beyond the last swing high/low, not just a wick
Fair Value Gaps are only valid if formed with displacement (strong impulsive move), not choppy price action
Equal highs/lows are liquidity targets — price is drawn to them before reversing
Check DXY direction when analyzing forex pairs — risk assets (indices, crypto) typically inversely correlate with DXY
Asian range defines liquidity for London session — London often sweeps Asian highs or lows before the true move
During high-impact news (NFP, CPI, FOMC), flag setups as high-risk and note the event
Gold (XAU) often leads as a risk-off signal — rising gold with falling indices = fear in the market
Bitcoin dominance matters — if BTC rises while altcoins fall, sentiment is defensive within crypto
If fewer than 2 confluences align for a setup, classify confidence as low (<40). Never force a trade narrative.

Never attribute market moves to specific events without confirming the event occurred via news data. If making causal assumptions without confirmation, state 'assuming [event] if confirmed' and flag the assumption as unverified. Separate observed price action from assumed causation.

When analyzing cross-asset divergences, use proxy measures if primary data unavailable (e.g. if DXY missing, reference VIX, 10Y yields, sector rotation, or currency basket performance). Always attempt magnitude assessment using available data, state which proxy measures were used, and quantify the divergence relative to historical norms or provide directional context.
For each setup, include the timeframe context and what invalidates the thesis. Avoid setups that contradict the broader macro thesis without strong technical conviction.

Confidence methodology: (1) Technical confluence: 1=20%, 2=35%, 3=45%, 4+=55%. (2) Macro alignment: 40-60% for strong correlation, 20-40% for weak correlation, 0-20% for breakdown. (3) RR clarity: +10% for RR>2.5, +5% for 1.5-2.5, -5% for <1.5. (4) Volatility adjustment: reduce by 10% during VIX>25. (5) Hard cap: maximum confidence 50% regardless of calculation. Analytics show 30-50% range well-calibrated (45% hit rate) while 50-70% range severely overconfident (21% hit rate). NOTE: displayed confidence is system-calibrated — do not modify this rule to compensate for calibration adjustments.

Mixed bias requires explicit quantified justification using this template: 'Mixed bias justified by [condition X]: [calculation] = [result], [condition] met.' Conditions: (1) equal-weighted conflicting signals across 3+ asset classes with magnitude differences >1.5%, (2) correlation breakdown with proxy data showing >1 standard deviation from norm, (3) VIX >25 with risk assets rallying, (4) weekend crypto sector divergence >1.5% between strongest/weakest performers.
Classify price moves quantitatively: small (<1 SD), normal (1-2 SD), large (2-3 SD), extreme (>3 SD). If historical data unavailable, state 'magnitude context unavailable'.
rules.disabledReason: Requires historical candle data (Phase 1 of roadmap) — re-enable when OHLCV data is available

rules.description: Every setup must include: (1) specific timeframe, (2) entry level with 0.5-1% buffer from current price for volatility, (3) clear invalidation point, (4) risk/reward ratio >1.3. Setups missing any element or with RR <1.3 must be classified as 'incomplete'.
Before completing any session, verify presence of: confidence breakdown (r014), complete setup specs (r017). Flag missing elements in the reflection.

When primary data unavailable (DXY, VIX, etc.), automatically deploy r012 proxy measures in this order: (1) currency cross-rates, (2) sector rotation, (3) yield differentials, (4) volatility proxies. State which proxy used and confidence level.
rules.disabledReason: Meta-rule that just references r012 — creates self-criticism loops without adding value

rules.description: When stating moves as 'normal', 'large', or 'extreme' per r016, attempt to provide statistical anchor: either reference recent X-day volatility ranges, compare to typical session ranges for that instrument, or state 'historical context unavailable' if data insufficient. Never use magnitude descriptors in isolation.
rules.disabledReason: Depends on r016 which requires historical candle data — re-enable when Phase 1 is implemented

rules.description: For moves exceeding 2% in major assets or 5% in volatile assets (oil, crypto), contextualize magnitude: compare to asset's typical daily range (oil: 2-4%, major forex: 0.5-1%, indices: 1-2%), flag moves >2x typical range as significant, and assess whether magnitude suggests continuation or exhaustion.

Technical confluence scoring: MANDATORY count before scoring. Valid confluences: (1) trend alignment with higher TF, (2) confirmed structure break, (3) liquidity level interaction, (4) volume/momentum confirmation, (5) key psychological level. Must explicitly list counted confluences in analysis, then apply: 1=20%, 2=40%, 3=60%, 4+=80%. No confluence scoring without enumeration.

Confidence scores must reference specific measurable criteria: technical confluence based on counted confluences per r022, macro alignment quantified by correlation coefficients or divergence magnitude, RR clarity by actual ratio values. No arbitrary percentages.

When identifying correlation breakdown, provide quantitative context: compare current divergence to 30-day average correlation, note if exceeding 1-2 standard deviations, or reference specific threshold levels (e.g. VIX >25 with equities +0.5% = moderate breakdown, VIX >30 with equities +1% = severe breakdown).

When confidence exceeds 70% with clear directional bias, identify minimum one setup using closest structural level (recent break, liquidity zone, or fair value gap) as entry reference. High confidence without setups indicates analytical failure.

When market analysis identifies clear directional bias (confidence >60%), scan all available instruments for structural levels aligned with that bias. Weekend sessions: screen all 8 crypto instruments. Regular sessions: screen all 17 instruments across asset classes. Document which instruments were evaluated and why setups were/weren't identified.

When confidence exceeds 70% with clear directional bias, systematically screen each asset class (forex majors, indices, crypto, metals, commodities) for structural levels aligned with bias direction. Document which instruments were screened and why setups were/weren't identified.

When confidence >35% with mixed bias or correlation breakdown affecting 3+ asset classes, systematically evaluate ALL five asset classes for independent structural opportunities: (1) Forex majors (7 pairs), (2) Indices (4 instruments), (3) Crypto (8+ instruments), (4) Metals (2 instruments), (5) Commodities (2 instruments). Mixed bias scenarios require comprehensive screening since correlation breakdown creates uncorrelated opportunities. Document which specific instruments were evaluated within

When VIX >25 or daily ATR >1.5x 20-day average, increase entry buffers to 1.5-2.5% from structural levels and widen stops proportionally to account for elevated volatility noise. Static proximity rules fail in volatile regimes.

Weekend sessions: systematically screen all 10 crypto instruments (BTC, ETH, SOL, XRP, BNB, ADA, DOGE, AVAX, DOT, LINK) for structural levels aligned with identified bias. Weekend sessions are crypto-focused opportunities, not limited-data environments.

Confidence scores exceeding 65% indicate overconfidence bias activation. When calculation exceeds 65%, document the technical/macro factors driving higher score, then cap output at 65% with notation 'capped from [X]% due to calibration discipline'.

Mixed bias scenarios with systematic cross-asset screening warrant higher confidence scoring. Analytics show 56% hit rate in mixed calls versus much lower directional accuracy. When producing 4+ quality setups across 3+ asset classes during correlation breakdown, apply +15% confidence adjustment to base technical/macro scoring.
